notes: Active intent map. Archive/legacy signals moved to records_archive_signals.json. USF observations default to low confidence until owner-confirmed.

records.title: Workspace Crate Role Split
records.summary: Repository is split into focused crates for engine, APIs, assets, mods, macros, and utils.
records.description: Crate responsibilities are documented and reflected in workspace layout.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in docs and root workspace files.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Engine Entrypoint Composes Runtime
records.summary: `core_engine` configures tracing/panic hooks, third-party Bevy plugins, `CoreApiPluginGroup`, then runs app.
records.description: Main binary is orchestration entrypoint and invokes core init function.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: CoreApiPluginGroup Is Primary Internal Composition Surface
records.summary: `CoreApiPluginGroup` registers workflow/core/config/debug/input/player/rhai/render/time/usf and related modules.
records.description: This is the principal module composition boundary for the runtime.
records.owner_direction: Keep as infrastructure baseline; semantics above it are redesignable.

records.title: core_mod_api Is Code-Only Internal Platform
records.summary: `core_mod_api` declares itself code-only and internal-first.
records.description: Assets are expected in companion asset crates, not in `core_mod_api`.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Build Pipeline Packages Profile Outputs
records.summary: Build scripts produce `build/<profile>` with executable, mod dylibs, and copied assets.
records.description: Profiles supported: `dev`, `fastdev`, `release`.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in shell scripts and build docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Build Uses Nightly And Separate Mod Build With init_api
records.summary: Main workspace build excludes mod crates, then mods are built separately with `--features init_api`.
records.description: This enforces explicit init symbol strategy for mod artifacts.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in build script and docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Run Script Configures Dynamic Library Resolution For Dev
records.summary: Dev/fastdev runs prepend target deps and Rust target lib dir to `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
records.description: Runtime launch path is `build/<profile>/core_engine`.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Docs Tree Separates Canonical vs Temp vs Legacy
records.summary: `docs/` is curated reference; `documents/temp_stuff` is non-canonical; `documents/legacy_stuff` is historical.
records.description: Repository explicitly distinguishes reference tiers.
records.owner_direction: USF redesign currently treats all repo USF guidance as observation-only until reapproved.

records.title: Architecture Doc Describes Dynamic Mod Loading Convention
records.summary: Architecture docs describe cdylib mods and init symbol convention for dynamic loading.
records.description: Current executable path still initializes core statically and leaves dynamic loading path commented.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in docs and main code.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: base_mod/base_mod_api Layers Exist
records.summary: Gameplay mod and API crates are present in workspace structure.
records.description: Current conversation centers on USF redesign; gameplay scope remains secondary.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in crate docs and source tree.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

future_depth.reason: Gameplay layer depth is likely to change during USF redesign.

records.title: Script Roots Are Separated By Role
records.summary: Core boot scripts, schedule entrypoint scripts, USF typed content, and TEMP ontology drafts are separated in `core_mod/assets/scripts`.
records.description: Current executable USF scripts and TEMP exploratory scripts coexist.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: AppSet Defines Ordered Global Runtime Phases
records.summary: Application-level orchestration chains Input->Intent->Simulation->BoundaryResolve->ChunkOrchestration->Camera->Presentation->Diagnostics.
records.description: Subsystems are expected to attach into these sets instead of ad-hoc ordering.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Workflow Plugin Is Central Staged Orchestration Infrastructure
records.summary: Workflow plugin is a core utility-side infrastructure capability for staged, high-throughput orchestration.
records.description: It initializes channels/resources, relays requests/responses, and bridges ECS/render stages. Workflows are intended for pipelineable/parallelizable heavy workloads in the engine-level utility layer.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Workflow Stage Taxonomy Is Explicit
records.summary: Stage families include `Ecs`, `Render`, `Async`, `EcsWhile`, and `RenderWhile`.
records.description: Stage contracts are generated/used by workflow macros and runtime types.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in workflow modules and style docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Workflow Timeout Default Is Panic-Fast
records.summary: Timeout handling emits a signal then panics in default run paths.
records.description: Abort/retry logic exists as control enums, but default path remains panic-fast.
records.owner_direction: Aligns with owner panic-fast preference.

records.title: Chunk Load Gate Is Config-Gated Run Condition
records.summary: Chunk-load gate can lock chunk processing and is enabled/disabled by config key `workflow/chunk_load_gate_enabled`.
records.description: Gate integrates with chunk orchestration run conditions.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in run condition logic and config.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Config Is Global Static Loaded From TOML
records.summary: Global CONFIG static loads `core_mod/configs/config.toml` through asset-root resolution.
records.description: Runtime code calls `CONFIG().get::<T>(...)` extensively.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in config statics and struct loader.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Config Flattening Uses Slash-Key Paths
records.summary: Nested TOML tables are flattened into slash-delimited key paths (e.g., `usf/active_modpack_id`).
records.description: This flattening defines the common runtime config lookup model.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Config Supports Domain Tree Merge
records.summary: Config loader merges extra TOML files under a `domains/` subtree in deterministic path order.
records.description: This provides extensible multi-file config composition.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in config merge code and domain files.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Config Getter Has Explicit Performance Design Concern
records.summary: Code notes a critical performance design flaw in dynamic typed config access and caching approach.
records.description: Potential future static/inlined config strategy is noted as TODO.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Active Modpack Default Is `debug`
records.summary: Default configured active USF modpack id is `debug` in root config.
records.description: Bootstrap reads this key to resolve active modpack.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in config and bootstrap constants.
records.owner_direction: USF semantics around this remain redesignable.

records.title: USF Chunk/Zone Realization Defaults Are Configured In Domain File
records.summary: Chunk realization defaults and zone realization window defaults are present in domain config files.
records.description: Current defaults set `levels_above_active=0` and `levels_below_active=0`.
records.owner_direction: Window semantics are likely to change with zone removal direction.

records.title: Main Runtime Uses Panic Hook And Optional Tracing Subscriber
records.summary: Main sets custom panic hook and optional tracing subscriber based on config/env.
records.description: Runtime observability and failure visibility are intentionally direct.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Boot Script Is Schedule Registration Only
records.summary: `boot.rhai` only registers schedule entrypoints and does not directly register USF content.
records.description: USF typed content registration is handled by dedicated bootstrap loader.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: USF Schedule Entrypoints Are Explicitly Split By Domain
records.summary: Schedule list includes `substrate_*`, `zone_*`, and `phenomenon_*` entrypoints.
records.description: Domain naming drives stage routing.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in boot script and bootstrap scheduler registration.
records.owner_direction: Zone domain portions are likely temporary under clean-slate direction.

records.title: Schedule Entrypoint Loader Uses Companion Ordering
records.summary: Companion files are loaded in deterministic suffix priority order before root entrypoint file.
records.description: Priority sequence: `.lib`, `.entrypoint`, `.substrate`, `.zone`, `.phenomenon`, then other `.rhai`.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Schedule Entrypoint Params Are Domain-Typed
records.summary: Entrypoint params are typed (`Global`, `Substrate`, `Zone`, `Phenomenon`) rather than generic map payloads.
records.description: Typed params include domain metadata and runtime counters.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in schedule entrypoint types and docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Schedule Params Include Bootstrap And Channel Telemetry
records.summary: Common params expose bootstrap script counts and chunk realization channel counters.
records.description: Scripts can inspect runtime state for diagnostics/orchestration decisions.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Typed USF Bootstrap Contract Defines Nine Script Types
records.summary: Bootstrap defines typed contract list for `mod`, `modpack`, `metric`, `zone`, `metric_set`, `zlm`, `scale`, `phenomenon`, `phenomenon_model`.
records.description: Each has explicit suffix, entrypoint, context type, and scope.
records.owner_direction: Contract set is expected to evolve under redesign.

records.title: USF Script Contracts Use Global And Package Scopes
records.summary: Script contracts separate global (`mod`, `modpack`) and package-scoped definitions for other USF types.
records.description: Scope labels are enforced in bootstrap processing.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Rhai Bootstrap Is Fail-Fast On Contract Violations
records.summary: Bootstrap and loader use panic-on-invalid contract/file/signature semantics.
records.description: This aligns with overall fail-fast scripting philosophy in current implementation.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed across loader code and docs.
records.owner_direction: Owner direction keeps panic-fast behavior.

records.title: Rhai Startup Test Harness Is Config-Gated
records.summary: Startup tests execute only when `rhai_binding/testing_enabled` is true.
records.description: Testing behavior is part of script/runtime integration policy.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Alias Preprocessor Is Partially Implemented
records.summary: `use ... as ...` alias preprocessing is active for boot/schedule scripts, with safety checks.
records.description: Typed helper and display shorthand proposals remain incomplete.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in ergonomics docs and scripting docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Generic-Like Dispatch Uses Signature Catalog + Resolver Policy
records.summary: Rhai generic-like behavior is resolved through reflected metadata, compile-time signature catalogs, and runtime dispatch keys.
records.description: Duplicate/invalid dispatch entries are policy errors.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in generic binding policy docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: AccessCell Provider Pattern Is Soundness Boundary
records.summary: `AccessCell` + provider patterns define runtime borrow/lifecycle safety boundary; misuse intentionally panics.
records.description: This model underpins bridge semantics.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Bridge Architecture Splits Domains And Runtime Wrappers
records.summary: Bridge declarations live under `bridges/domains/*` and wrappers under `runtime/*`.
records.description: Separation is explicit in scripting docs and dialect docs.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: Rhai Capability Coverage Is Broad But Incomplete
records.summary: Current bridge/runtime surface is substantial but not complete for full desired capability graph.
records.description: Roadmap docs and temp notes both indicate expansion remains.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

coverage_extent.reason: Coverage status is partially qualitative and still changing.

records.title: Startup Test Harness Has Structured Script Subtree
records.summary: Startup tests/examples live under dedicated schedule companion subtree.
records.description: Harness is intentionally separated from gameplay content scripts.
records.owner_direction: No direct contradiction.

records.title: USF Implementation Snapshot: Modular but Legacy/Zone-Centric

records.description: Observed module composition is still zone-centric and mainly useful as migration context.

records.title: USF Simulation Order Is Substrate -> Zone -> Phenomenon

records.description: Per-domain pre/runtime/post subsets are also chained.

records.title: Active Modpack Validation Is Strict And Panic-Oriented
records.summary: Current validation paths are strict and should fail fast on unexpected or unwanted failures.
records.description: Non-panic paths should exist only for explicitly intentional sentinel/control-flow errors that are meaningfully interpreted (for example reconciliation semantics).

records.title: Scale-First Foundation Signal (Current Code Only Partially Reflects It)
records.summary: Core direction is scale-first: all core USF concepts should be fundamentally structured around the locked 71-scale model.
records.description: Current code only shows a narrow slice of this via per-scale routing fields; target intent is broader (USF math/custom-stdlib foundation usable from Rust and exposed to scripting).

records.title: Scale-Window Chunk Targeting Uses Active Radius Plus Configurable Ancestor Closure
records.summary: Target contract: chunk targeting is active-scale neighborhood plus an explicit, configurable ancestor-scale closure window.
records.description: USF scale-window behavior is defined from scale semantics first, not current code shape. Active+above remains canonical, and ancestor-scale chunk load depth/count is a first-class configuration knob.

records.title: USF State Is Scale-Dependent Across Loaded Scales
records.summary: Target USF contract: all USF state domains are scale-dependent and reconciled across the loaded scale window.
records.description: This applies to Metrics, Phenomena, and additional USF-level domains as they emerge. Persistence/evolution semantics are scale-aware by design. Logic/state may run in optimized local numeric domains, but must be convertible into representations that preserve USF scale semantics.
records.current_implementation_observation: Current docs/code only show partial hints (mainly phenomenon-side reconcile/interaction), and remain non-authoritative for final design.
records.owner_direction: High-confidence direction: fundamental USF state/logic revolves around locked scale principles, with Bevy/ECS as execution substrate and room for future script-level USF mechanics.

records.title: Chunk Realization Is Intent-Driven Across Representation/Projection/Interaction Substrate Domains
records.summary: Target USF contract: chunk realization is intent-driven, with scripts orchestrating capability-channel pipelines across representation/projection/interaction substrate domains.

records.title: Bootstrap Descent Controller Defines Genesis/Evolve World Initialization Contract
records.summary: Target USF contract: a bootstrap descent controller governs initial Genesis sweep and subsequent Evolve catch-up across scales to the requested timeline.
records.description: Operational/controller realization of the higher-level Genesis/Evolve split principle (R-0061): Genesis creates irreversible base-state structure per seed/content, then Evolve advances state with script-defined dynamics under shared macro laws. The controller owns scale progression/stabilization gates and timeline catch-up policy.

records.title: Scale Spine: 71 Canonical Scales Are Core USF Structure
records.summary: Target USF contract: the system is organized around a fixed 71-scale spine.
records.description: Scale is a foundational axis across USF state, logic, and data representation. The locked 71-scale spine is a core concept-level backbone, and scales are treated as strict singleton content assets: exactly one per scale index, with no duplicates and no missing entries.
records.current_implementation_observation: Current scale module encodes a 71-level structure with exponent/index mapping helpers.

records.title: Cross-Scale Scalar Arithmetic Is An Explicit Math-Boundary Primitive (Partial Contract)
records.summary: Target math direction (partial): USF global scalar math is explicit and interoperable with local numeric regimes through deliberate conversions.
records.description: USFScalar-like constructs represent only a small fraction of the intended USF math contract. Most within-scale execution should still prefer cheaper local numerics, but global USF math may also be used inside local logic where needed. The key contract is explicit, meaningful conversion in both directions between global USF math types and local engine numeric types.
records.current_implementation_observation: Current scalar module provides a concrete but non-authoritative example of digit-stack cross-scale arithmetic and window conversion.
records.owner_direction: Use this as a partial anchor for broader USF global-math contracts and conversion-focused API design, not as a strict boundary-only rule or the full target math model.

sources.ref: conversation: rewrite as a small fraction of intended target math-boundary/general math contracts
conversation: global USF math is not boundary-only; local logic may still need it; conversions to/from local numeric types must be explicit and meaningful

usage_policy.reason: Preference toward local numerics is clear, but exact policy for when global math is required inside local logic is not yet formalized.

records.title: Owner Direction: USF Math Boundaries Must Be Explicit
records.summary: Cross-scale/global data and logic should use explicit scale-aware USF math primitives; inside-scale simulation should prefer native numerics by default.

Boundary points may also exist inside USF itself (between domains/mechanisms), and outputs may need USF-form representations even if internal calculation paths vary.

usage_topology.reason: Exact placement of global-USF math boundaries (global<->local and intra-USF domain interfaces) is not yet formally mapped.

records.title: Owner Direction: Scripts Own Policy, Substrate Executes Capabilities
records.summary: Principle-level rule: policy/control/intelligence live in scripts, while substrate remains structural and capability-execution oriented.
records.description: This is the governance/control-plane principle. The concrete orchestration shape for this principle is captured in R-0064 (capability-channel pipelines), and invocation governance is captured in R-0066.
records.owner_direction: Keep script-driven intelligence with minimal structural substrate; operationalize through R-0064.

sources.ref: conversation: scripts are smart, substrate is dumb
conversation: rust capabilities exposed and orchestrated in scripts

records.title: Owner Direction: Capability Channels Operationalize Script-Orchestration
records.summary: Operational model for R-0063/R-0066: capability channels are composable script-orchestrated pipelines with explicit scale-domain differentiation as a core requirement.
records.description: Rust defines/implements engine-level capabilities; scripts route and compose intent through capability channels across representation/projection/interaction domains (for example physics, audio, particles, pathfinding, IO, and related systems). Capability channels operate in one mode: they differentiate scales as channel layers of the same canonical/global space, with channel-specific handling details.
records.current_implementation_observation: Conversation direction; partially reflected by early channel-family realization code.
records.owner_direction: Preserve capability-channel pipelining; use script-authored capability invocation by default (R-0066); and require every capability to support scale-aware layer differentiation representing the same canonical/global space.

sources.ref: conversation: capability channel wording and pipelining implications are desirable
conversation: scripts orchestrate rust-defined engine-level capabilities through USF
conversation: capability channels should each implement subregioning in their own way with scale domain differentiation as a core requirement
conversation: capabilities should differentiate scales as layers of the same things in the same global/canonical space; no separate operational-mode split
conversation: not 100% sure yet which capabilities will exist/be needed
conversation: capabilities should be script-authored by default; direct rust exceptions may exist if sensible

contract_elaboration.reason: Capability-channel interface boundaries and composition primitives remain underdefined.

capability_surface.reason: Exact capability inventory is still open and expected to evolve.

invocation_governance.reason: Script-first invocation is clear, but exception scope and enforcement model are not yet formalized.

layer_semantics.reason: Per-capability semantics for layer identity, simulation cadence, and composition remain underdefined.

records.title: Owner Direction: Recursive Sub-Phenomena Are A First-Class Interface
records.summary: Phenomena may recursively decompose into sub-Phenomena so simulation/representation/interaction can operate on partial regions.
records.description: This is locked as high-level USF interface intent, not a single low-level mechanism. Sub-Phenomena recursion drives capability-channel work, but each capability may realize decomposition/layering differently.
records.current_implementation_observation: Conversation direction; current implementation does not yet formalize this interface.
records.owner_direction: Treat recursive sub-Phenomena semantics as core USF intent and keep mechanism details channel-specific.

sources.ref: conversation: subregioning is complex/uncertain and should be a high-level interface
conversation: game/engine is recursive subregioning of phenomena via further phenomena
conversation: subregioning is a generalization; phenomena as sub-phenomena is the specific framing

contract_elaboration.reason: Core intent is locked, but interface semantics and lifecycle boundaries remain underdefined.

records.title: Owner Direction: Capability Invocation Is Script-Authored By Default
records.summary: Core direction: scripts author USF content and are the default invocation surface for capabilities.
records.description: Capabilities should be consumed through script-authored USF concepts (for example Phenomena, Metrics, and PhenomenaRealizers), not as direct Rust-side gameplay paths by default. Narrow direct-Rust exceptions are allowed when justified.
records.owner_direction: Treat script-authored capability invocation as primary; allow sensible exceptions explicitly.

sources.ref: conversation: scripts fully author content and utilize capabilities
conversation: capabilities should explicitly/exclusively be used by scripts, with exceptions when they make sense

exception_scope.reason: Exceptions are allowed, but criteria/guardrails for direct Rust-side invocation are not yet formalized.

records.title: Owner Direction: Replay Is Deferred Due To Feasibility Uncertainty
records.summary: Replay is intentionally deferred because current target architecture is not assumed to support it cleanly without major changes.
records.description: Replay constraints should not drive current core USF design. Replay may be revisited later if feasibility improves, but it is currently a high-uncertainty, high-cost candidate and may remain unimplemented.
records.owner_direction: Defer replay strongly and treat it as optional/uncertain until architecture maturity justifies re-evaluation.

sources.ref: conversation: unsure replay is feasible without extensive changes; defer heavily and possibly never implement

long_term_inclusion.reason: Owner is uncertain replay can be supported without significant architectural changes and may choose not to implement it.

records.title: Owner Direction: Script Failures Should Panic Fast
records.summary: Owner prefers panic-fast as the default failure policy for script/runtime failures.
records.description: Default stance is fail-fast panic for unexpected/unwanted failures. Non-panic paths are allowed only as explicit intentional sentinel/error branches, not hidden fallback behavior.
records.owner_direction: Keep panic-fast as default with explicit intentional exceptions only.

sources.ref: conversation: script failures should panic fast
conversation: unexpected failures panic; sentinel-path errors may be non-panic
conversation: default should be panic-fast, not a 100%-always mandatory mode

exception_policy.reason: Intentional non-panic exception taxonomy and enforcement mechanism are not yet fully formalized.

records.title: Divergence: Zone-Centric Runtime/Docs Vs Remove-Zones Target
records.summary: Current implementation and canonical docs remain strongly zone-centric.
records.description: Owner target model removes zones as first-class concept.
records.owner_direction: Resolve by defining zone replacement vocabulary and authority path.

records.title: Divergence: Below-Active Window Support Vs Strict Active+Above
records.summary: Current code still supports below-active windows, but target policy is locked to active+above only.
records.description: This is an explicit implementation lag, not an open direction question.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in zone resources and owner direction.
records.owner_direction: Lock active+above only.

records.title: Divergence: Child Finer-Scale Request Paths Vs Active+Above-Only Simulation
records.summary: Current flow references child finer-scale request paths, but owner direction forbids below-active active/full simulation.
records.description: No exceptions: scales below the currently active scale are never actively or fully simulated.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in flow docs + owner direction.
records.owner_direction: Lock active+above-only simulation; disallow below-active active/full simulation.

sources.ref: conversation: no exceptions; below-active scales are never actively/fully simulated

records.title: Divergence: Determinism Language Vs Statistical Consistency Direction
records.summary: Prototype docs still demand deterministic equal-seed behavior.
records.description: Owner direction reframes this as macro-law consistency with stochastic drift over timeline.
records.owner_direction: Requires doc and contract rewrite.

determinism_semantics.reason: This remains actively debated and needs precise formal language.

records.title: Contract Target: Genesis and Evolve Must Be Distinct Lifecycle Phases
records.summary: Genesis defines initial world-state creation; evolve defines ongoing world-state progression under the same macro laws.
records.description: Target contract: genesis and evolve are first-class, non-interchangeable phases with explicit boundaries.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in worldgen code and owner discussion.
records.owner_direction: Formalize the genesis/evolve contract in explicit records/spec terms.

sources.ref: conversation: genesis/evolve split must be formalized

records.title: Gap: Post-Zone Replacement Vocabulary Is Unlocked
records.summary: Zone removal is decided, but replacement canonical terminology (e.g., realizer naming) is not yet locked.
records.description: Current terms appear exploratory and inconsistent.
records.owner_direction: Needs controlled vocabulary decision.

sources.ref: conversation: possible phenomena_realizer mention + remove zones

records.title: Gap: Script-First Capability Invocation Governance Is Not Formalized
records.summary: Script-authored capability invocation is locked directionally, but exception policy and enforcement mechanics are not yet specified.
records.description: Open questions include allowed direct-Rust exception paths, required justification/visibility for exceptions, and enforcement points that keep script-first invocation as the default contract.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed from owner direction and current implementation lag signals.
records.owner_direction: Define explicit invocation governance contract: script-first default, exception scope, and enforcement model.

records.title: Gap: Timescale Model Decoupled From Zones Is Not Formalized
records.summary: Current timescale helper lives under zone module, but target model removes zones.
records.description: A zone-less temporal contract is not documented yet.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed from code and owner direction.
records.owner_direction: Needs new first-class timescale contract.

sources.ref: conversation: remove zones + timescale coupling still important

records.title: Gap: Formal Seed/Content/Timeline Semantics Are Not Codified
records.summary: Owner direction defines macro-law consistency with drift, but formal contract terms for seed/content/timeline interaction are not yet written.
records.description: No precise metrics/acceptance criteria currently specified.
records.owner_direction: Needs explicit formalism records.

sources.ref: conversation: same seed+content+timeline should keep macro laws with drift

formal_spec.reason: Target semantics are conceptual but not formally parameterized yet.

records.title: Gap: Logic-Entity Vs Representation-Entity Split Contract Is Not Formalized
records.summary: Owner direction requires an explicit phenomenon backend/frontend split (logic entities vs representation entities), but both sides are still concretely underdefined.
records.description: This split is needed for multi-scale local composition in shared Bevy-space, but remains a distinct responsibility from recursive subregioning. We have rough ideas/signals for how to pull it off, yet concrete logic-side and representation-side contracts (ownership, sync cadence, lifecycle, projection, and mapping rules) are still open.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed from owner discussion; current records/code do not define a formal split contract.
records.owner_direction: Specify this contract independently from recursive subregioning semantics.

sources.ref: conversation: multi-scale composition needs explicit logic/backend vs rendering/frontend entity split
conversation: do not mix entity split and recursive subregioning as one responsibility

formalization.reason: Contract boundaries and mechanics are acknowledged but still largely undefined.

records.title: Gap: Capability Scale-Layer Semantics And Invariants Are Not Formalized
records.summary: Direction is locked that capabilities differentiate scales as layers of the same canonical/global space, but concrete layer semantics/invariants are not yet specified.
records.description: Open questions include per-capability layer identity rules, layer simulation cadence, layer-to-layer interaction/composition rules, and required invariants each capability must expose for coherent scale orchestration.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed from owner discussion; no formal per-capability scale-layer invariants are currently recorded.
records.owner_direction: Define explicit per-capability scale-layer semantics and invariants under a single canonical-space layering model.

sources.ref: conversation: capabilities each need independently-simulated separated layers and scale differentiation
conversation: layers represent the same things in the same global/canonical space; no separate operational-mode split
conversation: this sounds like a good unresolved question

capability_inventory.reason: Target capability set is not finalized, so required invariants must remain capability-agnostic for now.

formalization.reason: Core direction exists, but concrete per-capability layer semantics and invariants are still undefined.

records.title: Owner Direction: Canonical USF Definitions Are Script-Declared At Startup And Then Frozen
records.summary: Scales, Metrics, Phenomena, and PhenomenaRealizers are declared in script-land during startup and become immutable for runtime updates.
records.description: Canonical USF definition contracts are loaded once at startup/pre-genesis. Runtime update phases may not register new canonical definitions.
records.owner_direction: Lock canonical definition authoring to startup and freeze definitions afterward.

sources.ref: conversation: canonical type definitions immutable after startup
conversation: no new canonical definitions during Update

records.title: Owner Direction: Update Phase Is For State Evolution And Intent Emission Only
records.summary: Update-time scripting is limited to evolving state and emitting intents under predeclared canonical contracts.
records.description: Canonical definition changes are startup-only. Update flow mutates runtime state through authorized state evolution and capability intent pathways.
records.owner_direction: Treat update as runtime evolution only; no definition registration.

sources.ref: conversation: during Update only evolve state/emit intents

records.title: Owner Direction: Scripts Emit Intents, Rust Capability Contracts Commit
records.summary: Scripts are intent authors; Rust-defined capability channels own merge/reconcile/apply semantics and final state commit.
records.description: Capability channels are Rust authority surfaces. Script-side APIs parameterize capability behavior but do not bypass Rust contract legality.
records.owner_direction: Keep intent/commit split with Rust owning merge/reconcile/apply and panic-fast failure handling.

sources.ref: conversation: emit intents processed by rust-side
conversation: rust-defined capability handles merge/reconcile/cancel and panics on failure
conversation: rust contract defines what scripts can and cannot do

records.title: Owner Direction: Capability Orchestration Must Be ECS-Native, Not Public Global Statics
records.summary: Gameplay capability orchestration should live in ECS systems/resources/events, not publicly accessible global statics.
records.description: Inert infra statics (for example globally loaded config) are acceptable, but orchestration for gameplay capabilities (graphics/audio/physics/pathfinding/AI/etc.) must remain ECS-native.
records.current_implementation_observation: Conversation direction; existing code still uses wider static patterns.
records.owner_direction: Remove static-global gameplay-capability coordination; keep orchestration in ECS scheduling/runtime surfaces.

sources.ref: conversation: do not create global statics for capability coordination; embrace Bevy ECS
conversation: config-like global static is fine, gameplay capability statics are not

migration_scope.reason: Existing static usages need classification into tolerated infra vs forbidden gameplay orchestration.

records.title: Owner Direction: One PhenomenaRealizer Contract Per Scale, Chunk-Parameterized Execution
records.summary: Each scale has one realizer definition; realization runs per chunk using chunk data as parameter/context.
records.description: Realizer cardinality is per scale, not per chunk. Chunks are large partitions and may host internal optimized spatial structures (including sub-chunk partitioning details) used during realization.
records.owner_direction: Lock one realizer definition per scale with chunk-parameterized realization semantics.

sources.ref: conversation: one realizer per scale; chunk is parameterization/input context
conversation: chunks act as parent partitions with internal sub-partition structures

partition_mechanics.reason: Exact chunk-internal partitioning strategy is directional (octree/smart leaves) but not finalized.

records.title: Owner Direction: Metrics And Phenomena Must Coexist In Persistence
records.summary: Both Metrics and Phenomena are required persistent state domains; neither is optional in save-state authority.
records.description: Phenomena may be rebuilt with expected fidelity/data loss, but persistence should keep both domains to preserve practical world continuity and detail representation.
records.owner_direction: Persist both Metrics and Phenomena as canonical coexisting world state.

sources.ref: conversation: phenomena and metrics must coexist even in persistence
conversation: rebuild may be possible but with significant loss

rebuild_policy.reason: Exact rebuild/recovery policy and acceptable loss thresholds are not yet formalized.

records.title: Owner Direction: Cross-Scale Access Requires Explicit Specialized APIs
records.summary: Any communication or mutation across scales must flow through explicit specialized interfaces.
records.description: Arbitrary direct cross-scale access is disallowed. Cross-scale coupling can be bidirectional, but only via explicit operations/contracts.
records.owner_direction: Treat cross-scale operations as explicit API-bound actions with clear contract semantics.

sources.ref: conversation: cross-scale communication should be explicit operations
conversation: working with other scales requires specialized interface/API

records.title: Owner Direction: Capability Channels Are Closed Typed Contracts With Bootstrap Dependency Validation
records.summary: Capability channels are predeclared typed engine contracts; compatibility/dependency mismatches fail fast at bootstrap.
records.description: Mods and phenomenon types bind against declared channel contracts and dependency graphs. Future engine-level capability extensions are allowed, but channel legality remains contract-driven and validated before runtime.
records.owner_direction: Keep channels closed/typed-by-contract and enforce dependency compatibility with panic-fast startup validation.

sources.ref: conversation: hardcode/contract capabilities as channels to prevent improper use
conversation: capability mismatch at bootstrap should panic immediately
conversation: capability channels declared at engine level; phenomenon types declare required capabilities

extension_model.reason: Future engine-level modded capability extension model is desired but not yet formalized.

records.title: Owner Direction: Capability Channels Use Explicit Dependency Graph Semantics
records.summary: Capability channels should declare explicit dependency/compatibility relations (for example required companion channels or equivalent providers).
records.description: Direction favors a channel-level dependency graph so capability requirements are explicit and validated at bootstrap. Invalid graphs, missing dependencies, and version mismatches should fail fast. Dependency semantics may include alternatives (for example one-of provider classes) rather than only hard one-to-one requirements.
records.owner_direction: Formalize capability-channel dependency graph semantics as bootstrap compatibility authority.

sources.ref: conversation: capability channels should encode dependencies between capabilities
conversation: compatibility/dependency mismatches should panic at bootstrap
conversation: correction — dependency-graph direction applies to capability channels, not script precedence

graph_model.reason: Exact channel dependency schema, alternative-provider semantics, and cycle policy are not yet formally specified.

records.title: Gap: Endgame-Backwards Capability Contract And Script-Type Demo Spec Is Missing
records.summary: A concrete endgame-driven demo spec (sample phenomena, script hooks, capability API usage) is still missing.
records.description: Owner direction is to design backwards from a target demo/content slice (similar to TEMP sketches) so script types, hooks/methods, parameter APIs, and global capability APIs are defined by demonstrated use-cases before implementation.
records.current_implementation_observation: Current records contain many principles but not a compact backwards-designed demo contract artifact.
records.owner_direction: Create a concrete target-demo design slice and derive capability contracts/script interfaces from it.

sources.ref: conversation: work backwards from endgame demo and TEMP sketches to define script hooks and capability APIs

demo_scope.reason: Exact first demo slice boundaries and selected phenomena are not yet locked.

records.title: Gap: Script/Module Dependency Graph Semantics Are Not Formalized
records.summary: Script/module package dependency and conflict semantics are still underdefined as a first-class contract.
records.description: Beyond capability-channel dependency graphs, script/module composition likely needs explicit dependency/conflict/version semantics and deterministic resolution rules. Current direction is exploratory and should stay separate from capability-channel graph authority.
records.current_implementation_observation: Conversation direction indicates possible need, but no formal contract is locked.
records.owner_direction: Keep as an open design gap while capability-channel dependency graph remains the primary locked direction.

sources.ref: conversation: script/package-manager-like dependency semantics were discussed but later corrected as not the primary dependency graph focus

scope.reason: Unclear whether script/module graph needs full package-manager semantics or a narrower execution-order/declaration model.
